insight: Bombarder was considered by several top players (including apooche, 307th, and msven) to be in need of a gold cost nerf, as it dominates sets it appears in with a repetitive 'buy as many as possible' strategy that is both boring and extremely set-defining.
author: silentslayers
context: Discussion of the January 2019 balance patch, which did not include a bombarder nerf despite community expectation.

insight: Masn6811 argued that Centurion and Thorium Dynamo are more problematic than Bombarder because they are 'unskippable' in over 90% of sets they appear in and lock the first three turns of the game into a near-deterministic opener, regardless of the other 10 units in the set.
author: masn6811
context: Expert discussion comparing the set-warping effects of Bombarder, Centurion, and Thorium Dynamo following the January 2019 patch.

insight: When Thorium Dynamo is in the set, the optimal opener is nearly always 'DD / D1 / DD .. DD / 11 / D' (drones turn 1, dynamo turn 2, drones turn 3) approximately 90-93% of the time, regardless of what the other 10 units in the set are.
author: masn6811
context: Masn6811 illustrating that Thorium Dynamo's power makes the early game nearly deterministic when it appears in the set.

insight: Masn6811 argued that Thorium Dynamo being 'more balanced than Trinity Drone' is a misleading framing: a unit being less imbalanced than a clearly broken unit does not make it well-balanced, analogizing it to a hypothetical 'Superaegis' unit being more balanced than Thorium Dynamo by the same logic.
author: masn6811
context: Debate about whether Thorium Dynamo is acceptably balanced relative to Trinity Drone.

insight: Amalloy encountered a bug in the browser version where Master Bot built a Rhino that was invisible until the player reset to the beginning of the turn; the Rhino's damage (manifested as 2 Tarsiers dealing 3 damage) was the only indication it existed.
author: amalloy
context: Amalloy reported an unusual rendering/display bug while playing the browser version against Master Bot.

insight: The blueprint/leveling system in Prismata was designed to gradually expose new players to units rather than overwhelming them with all 80+ units immediately; in casual and bot games, only unlocked units appear in the random set, while ranked games use all units regardless of level.
author: apooche
context: Apooche explained the design intent of the blueprint leveling system in response to a new player's confusion about deck construction.
